sections.paragraph: Eze 47:16, and בֵּרֹותַי 2Sa 8:8 (“my wells,” for בִּאֵרֹותַי) [Berothah, Berothai], pr.n. of a town rich in brass, which was formerly subject to the kings of Zobah, situated on the northern borders of Palestine. Some understand Berytus [Beirout], a maritime city of Phœnicia, but from Eze 1:1. this city appears not to have been on the sea coast, but rather in the neighbourhood of Hamath; see Rosenm. Alterthumsk, ii. page 292.
